findings.description: Top branding graphic is reduced and repositioned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the USDA logo and department wordmark appear prominently near the upper left above the table. In the HTML, the logo is smaller and sits within a header band with more surrounding whitespace, altering its visual prominence.

findings.description: Text styling differs, including loss of color emphasis and highlighted row styling.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses colored header text (red/green), blue state names, and a yellow highlight on the North Carolina row. The HTML renders the table in mostly uniform grayscale without the color cues or row highlight.

findings.description: One table row label is truncated in the HTML, causing loss of table data fidelity.
findings.evidence: In the PDF table, rank 48 shows the state/jurisdiction as "District of Columbia". In the HTML table, the corresponding row is rendered as "District of" with values 15.74 | 15.87 | 14.53 | 48, omitting "Columbia".

findings.description: Text accuracy issue due to truncated jurisdiction name.
findings.evidence: PDF displays the full entry "District of Columbia". HTML shows only "District of", which changes the proper noun and loses meaning.
